🏗️ IPO Pipeline Architecture

Phase 1 — INPUT (Fidelity)

  • Missing Data Decision Matrix
  • KNN / Median / Mode Imputation
  • IQR Outlier Detection
  • numpy.clip() Winsorization
  • Distribution Variance Guard

Phase 2 — PROCESS (Engine)

  • Vectorized Date Feature Extraction
  • Revenue Feature Engineering
  • Label Encoding (Ordinal)
  • One-Hot Encoding (Nominal)
  • Pearson Collinearity Eradication

Phase 3 — OUTPUT (Contracts)

  • 3 Engineered Predictive Features
  • Runtime Schema Contracts
  • Point-in-Time Temporal Split
  • Offline Feature Store Push
  • Online Feature Cache Sync

📈 Visual Analysis

Missing Values Analysis

CouponCode has 309 missing values (25.8%). All other features are complete. Strategy: categorical mode-fill applied.

Numeric Feature Distributions

Histograms and box plots for all 4 numeric features. TotalPrice shows right skew with 8 detected outliers.

Categorical Feature Distributions

All categorical features are well-balanced across classes — no dominant category bias detected.

Pearson Correlation Matrix (Raw)

Low inter-feature correlation in raw data. Engineered features (EffectiveUnitPrice, Quarter) later found collinear and removed.

Revenue Analysis by Segment

Median TotalPrice is similar across products. Instagram leads in total revenue contribution among referral sources.

Outlier Handling: IQR Winsorization

IQR bounds: [−1341, 3330]. 8 values clipped via numpy.clip(). Row count preserved — no data dropped.

Engineered Features — Visual Validation

All 3 engineered features show meaningful variance. SpendTier: 609 Low / 317 Medium / 180 High / 94 Premium.

Monthly Revenue Trend (2023–2025)

Revenue data spans 30 months. Temporal train/test split applied at 80/20 cutoff to prevent data leakage.

Collinearity Heatmap — Before Removal

Two collinear pairs detected above |r|=0.8: UnitPrice↔EffectiveUnitPrice (r=1.0) and Month↔Quarter (r=0.97).

Collinearity Heatmap — After Removal

After dropping EffectiveUnitPrice and Quarter, no pairs exceed the |r|=0.8 threshold.

🔧 Engineered Features Summary

Feature 1: RevenueIntensityScore

TotalPrice / (Quantity × ItemsInCart)

Captures revenue per unit of cart activity. High score = premium low-volume order. Fully vectorized via numpy.where().

Feature 2: PriceDeviation

UnitPrice − CategoryMean(UnitPrice)

Signals pricing above/below category average. Computed via pandas groupby.transform() — zero Python loops.

Feature 3: SpendTier

pd.cut(TotalPrice, bins=4, labels=[0,1,2,3])

Ordinal spend tier: Low(609) / Medium(317) / High(180) / Premium(94). Useful as a stratification key or proxy target.
